tasks.phaseName: P1 â€” The construction seam (accessor pair)
tasks.subject: [capture-cloud-link-stub] [P1] Reseat cloudLinkManager to init/getCloudLinkManager, route routes/cloud.ts (4 sites) + index.ts (import/boot/stop) through the accessor, update the cloud.test.ts mock, seam construction unit tests
tasks.activeForm: Reseating cloudLinkManager to the init/getCloudLinkManager accessor pair

tasks.phaseName: P2 â€” The fake FetchLike + composition-root wiring + unreachability
tasks.subject: [capture-cloud-link-stub] [P2] Add createFakeCloudLinkFetch() (four scripted endpoints, pinned constants, per-device-code auto-flip, throwing guard) at fake-cloud-link.ts, wire it via gated dynamic import in index.ts, fake behavior + guard + import-graph tests
tasks.description: Add a `DORKOS_TEST_RUNTIME`-only fake `FetchLike` next to `demo-scenarios.ts`, wire it into the composition root behind the existing gate, and prove â€” structurally and by test â€” that it can never reach a production build. THE FAKE MODULE (new file apps/server/src/services/runtimes/test-mode/fake-cloud-link.ts) â€” mirror `demo-scenarios.ts`'s module-doc honesty pattern ("These exist ONLY to stage beautiful, truthful UI â€¦ reachable only when `DORKOS_TEST_RUNTIME=true` â€¦ Nothing here is wired into production", demo-scenarios.ts:7-15) and `test-control.ts`'s unreachability doc ("Only mounted when DORKOS_TEST_RUNTIME=true. Returns 404 â€¦ in production", test-control.ts:11-12). Import `type { FetchLike, DeviceCodeResponse } from '../../core/auth/cloud-link-client.js'` (type-only, erased at runtime â€” the fakeâ†’prod direction is allowed even though prod never imports the fake) and `import { env } from '../../../env.js';`. Pinned deterministic constants (module-level, no `Date.now()` in anything a screenshot shows) â€” copy exactly: `const FAKE_USER_CODE = 'DORK-2F7Q';` (pending code shown in the panel), `const FAKE_ACCOUNT_LABEL = 'Dork Labs';` (account label shown linked), `const FAKE_DEVICE_CODE = 'fake-device-code';` (opaque, never shown), `const FAKE_ACCESS_TOKEN = 'fake-instance-key';` (persisted at `cloud.instanceToken`, never shown/logged), `const FAKE_INSTANCE_ID = 'capture-instance';` (echoed by heartbeat), `const DEVICE_CODE_INTERVAL_SECONDS = 1;` (server poll cadence), `const DEVICE_CODE_EXPIRES_IN_SECONDS = 900;` (long enough to never visibly expire mid-capture), `const PENDING_POLLS_BEFORE_APPROVAL = 2;` (token polls answered `authorization_pending` before approval) â€” each carrying the one-line TSDoc comment given above, verbatim. THE FACTORY â€” copy the full function verbatim from spec Detailed Design Â§2: `export function createFakeCloudLinkFetch(): FetchLike { if (!env.DORKOS_TEST_RUNTIME) { throw new Error('createFakeCloudLinkFetch is test-mode only (DORKOS_TEST_RUNTIME)'); } const pollCounts = new Map<string, number>(); return async (input, init) => { const { pathname } = new URL(input); const json = (status: number, body: unknown) => new Response(JSON.stringify(body), { status, headers: { 'content-type': 'application/json' } }); if (pathname.endsWith('/api/auth/device/code')) { return json(200, { device_code: FAKE_DEVICE_CODE, user_code: FAKE_USER_CODE, verification_uri: 'https://dorkos.ai/activate', verification_uri_complete: `https://dorkos.ai/activate?code=${FAKE_USER_CODE}`, expires_in: DEVICE_CODE_EXPIRES_IN_SECONDS, interval: DEVICE_CODE_INTERVAL_SECONDS } satisfies DeviceCodeResponse); } if (pathname.endsWith('/api/auth/device/token')) { const { device_code } = JSON.parse(String(init?.body ?? '{}')) as { device_code?: string }; const key = device_code ?? FAKE_DEVICE_CODE; const seen = pollCounts.get(key) ?? 0; pollCounts.set(key, seen + 1); if (seen < PENDING_POLLS_BEFORE_APPROVAL) { return json(400, { error: 'authorization_pending' }); } return json(200, { access_token: FAKE_ACCESS_TOKEN }); } if (pathname.endsWith('/api/instances/heartbeat')) { return json(200, { instanceId: FAKE_INSTANCE_ID, lastSeenAt: new Date().toISOString(), accountLabel: FAKE_ACCOUNT_LABEL }); } if (pathname.endsWith('/api/instances/revoke')) { return json(200, {}); } throw new Error(`fake cloud-link: unexpected request ${pathname}`); }; }` â€” the guard checks `env.DORKOS_TEST_RUNTIME` (the VALIDATED boolean from `apps/server/src/env.ts:90-93`, `z.string().optional().transform(v => v === 'true')`), never a raw string comparison like `!== 'true'` (a correction the SPECIFY stage made to the ideation). Poll counting is per `device_code` (`pollCounts` keyed by the code), so a future unlinkâ†’relink cycle resets deterministically. An unknown pathname throws â€” fail loud, never a silent escape. COMPOSITION-ROOT WIRING (apps/server/src/index.ts) â€” in the `if (env.DORKOS_TEST_RUNTIME) { â€¦ }` branch of the runtime-registration block (:440-469), REPLACE the interim `initCloudLinkManager();` call task 1.1 added with: `const { createFakeCloudLinkFetch } = await import('./services/runtimes/test-mode/fake-cloud-link.js'); initCloudLinkManager({ fetchImpl: createFakeCloudLinkFetch() });` â€” placed after the existing `TestModeRuntime` registration lines, mirroring that block's own dynamic-import pattern (`const { TestModeRuntime } = await import('./services/runtimes/test-mode/test-mode-runtime.js');`). Dynamic `import()` keeps `fake-cloud-link.ts` OUT of the production module graph â€” exactly like `TestModeRuntime` and the test-control reset path (test-control.ts:44-50). Leave the `else` branch's `initCloudLinkManager();` (from task 1.1, real fetch, real defaults) UNCHANGED â€” it already satisfies "production `else` branch calls `initCloudLinkManager()` (no options)". This still runs before `getCloudLinkManager().initOnStartup()` (index.ts:1430) and any request, so the accessor's guard never trips; in test mode the isolated capture `config.json` holds no `cloud.instanceToken`, so `initOnStartup()` returns early (cloud-link.ts:240-241) and never heartbeats until the drive links. FAKE BEHAVIOR + GUARD + IMPORT-GRAPH TESTS (new file apps/server/src/services/runtimes/test-mode/__tests__/fake-cloud-link.test.ts) â€” with `env.DORKOS_TEST_RUNTIME` stubbed true (e.g. `vi.mock('../../../../env.js', ...)` or direct property stub, matching this repo's existing env-stubbing pattern in other test-mode tests): (a) `device/code` request â†’ 200 with the pinned `user_code`, `verification_uri: 'https://dorkos.ai/activate'`, and `interval: 1` â€” purpose: pinned, deterministic pending content. (b) `device/token` â†’ `400 {error: 'authorization_pending'}` for the first `PENDING_POLLS_BEFORE_APPROVAL` (2) calls to the returned `FetchLike`, then `200 {access_token: FAKE_ACCESS_TOKEN}` on the 3rd â€” purpose: the offline auto-flip, deterministically. (c) `device/token` counting is per `device_code` â€” driving a second, distinct `device_code` starts its own count from 0 â€” purpose: relink determinism, no cross-cycle bleed. (d) `heartbeat` â†’ 200 with the pinned `accountLabel` and a fresh ISO `lastSeenAt` â€” purpose: the linked view shows the account + "just now". (e) `revoke` â†’ 200 â€” purpose: best-effort no-op covered. (f) an unknown pathname â†’ throws â€” purpose: fail loud, never a silent escape. GUARD unit (same file): (g) `createFakeCloudLinkFetch()` throws when `env.DORKOS_TEST_RUNTIME` is false; (h) returns a callable `FetchLike` when true. IMPORT-GRAPH unit (same file or a sibling): (i) read the source text of `cloud-link.ts`, `cloud-link-client.ts`, and `routes/cloud.ts` (e.g. `fs.readFileSync`) and assert none contain the string `'fake-cloud-link'` â€” purpose: the structural half of unreachability, asserted rather than assumed. Each test carries a one-line purpose comment; no always-pass tests. Acceptance: `fake-cloud-link.ts` exists with the exact pinned constants and factory shape; `index.ts`'s `DORKOS_TEST_RUNTIME` branch wires the fake via gated dynamic import; the `else` branch is untouched from task 1.1; all 9 test cases (6 behavior + 2 guard + 1 import-graph) pass. Verify: pnpm --filter @dorkos/server typecheck && pnpm --filter @dorkos/server lint && pnpm vitest run apps/server/src/services/runtimes/test-mode/__tests__/fake-cloud-link.test.ts. Cites: spec Â§Detailed Design 2 (the fake, exact code block and pinned constants), Â§Detailed Design 3 (composition-root wiring, exact code block), Â§Detailed Design 4 (the three layered unreachability defenses), Â§Decisions resolved ("The throwing guard checks the validated boolean, not the raw string. RESOLVED"), Â§Testing Strategy (fake behavior unit, guard unit, import-graph unit), Â§Code structure & file organization table. Depends on 1.1 (needs `initCloudLinkManager`/`getCloudLinkManager` to exist before the composition-root wiring can call them, and `CloudLinkManagerOptions`/`FetchLike` are already exported from the seam's neighborhood).
tasks.activeForm: Adding the fake FetchLike and wiring it under the DORKOS_TEST_RUNTIME gate

tasks.phaseName: P3 â€” The capture drive (shots + shard pin)
tasks.subject: [capture-cloud-link-stub] [P3] Register accounts-pending + accounts-linked (pinned to SHARD_0_PINNED_SHOTS), update the shots.test.ts pin-equality assertion, add the shootCloudLink drive and its guarded call at the end of captureLightStills
tasks.activeForm: Registering the two shots and adding the shootCloudLink drive

tasks.phaseName: P4 â€” Capture run + docs embeds
tasks.subject: [capture-cloud-link-stub] [P4] Run capture record+process to publish accounts-pending/accounts-linked + the manifest, embed both via <ProductShot> in the two "Linking from Settings" Steps of dorkos-accounts.mdx, confirm the site docs-embed guard is green
tasks.description: Produce the real assets from the drive built in 3.1, then land the docs embeds that depend on them â€” this task is explicitly sequenced last among the code/asset work because the site's docs-embed guard cannot pass until the PNGs and manifest exist. CAPTURE RUN â€” from the repo root, run `pnpm --filter @dorkos/e2e capture:record` (boots an isolated `DORK_HOME`, a test-mode server + Vite client, drives every registered shot including the new `shootCloudLink` flow from 3.1, and saves raw recordings/screenshots into `apps/e2e/capture/library/<run-id>/raw/`), then `pnpm --filter @dorkos/e2e capture:process` (edits raws + applies any overrides into `apps/site/public/product/`, regenerating `apps/site/public/product/manifest.json` via `writeManifest` with the registry snapshot). Equivalently, `pnpm --filter @dorkos/e2e capture` runs both phases. Confirm the run output reports `accounts-pending` and `accounts-linked` captured (not skipped) â€” if either is skipped, check `SHARD_0_PINNED_SHOTS`/`isShotSkipped` overrides from 3.1 and the `DORKOS_TEST_RUNTIME`-gated fake wiring from 2.1 both landed. Expect `apps/site/public/product/accounts-pending-light.png` and `apps/site/public/product/accounts-linked-light.png` to exist after the process phase (both `kind: 'still'`, so no `-dark.webm` is expected â€” only loops publish a video). DOCS EMBEDS (docs/self-hosting/dorkos-accounts.mdx) â€” the "Linking from Settings" section has two `<Step>`s. `ProductShot` is a global MDX component (verified: no import line exists anywhere across `docs/**`, so no import is needed here). After the "Link this instance" step's body ("Click **Link this instance**. The panel shows the same 8-character code and an **Open dorkos.ai/activate** button (the code is pre-filled)."), add on its own line: `<ProductShot id="accounts-pending" alt="Settings â†’ DorkOS account showing the 8-character link code and the 'Open dorkos.ai/activate' button, waiting for approval" />`. After the "Approve, and watch it flip" step's body ("Approve at `/activate`. The panel updates **in place**, from _pending_ to _linked_, showing the linked account, the instance name, and the last heartbeat. An expired code offers **Generate a new code**."), add: `<ProductShot id="accounts-linked" alt="The same Settings panel flipped to Linked â€” a green dot, the linked account, and 'Last synced just now'" />`. Do not touch any other section of the file (the `dorkos cloud` CLI steps above, or the "instance registry"/"Revoking an instance" sections below, are all out of scope â€” the site `/account/instances` table is explicitly DOR-283-parked). VERIFY THE GUARD â€” the load-bearing check is `apps/site/src/layers/features/marketing/lib/__tests__/shots.test.ts`'s `describe('docs ProductShot embeds', ...)` block: it scans every `<ProductShot id="â€¦">` under `docs/**`, and for each asserts (a) the id is registered in the published manifest (`getProductShot(id)` truthy), (b) `<id>-light.png` exists under `apps/site/public/product/`, and (c) the shot's `consumers` includes `'docs'`. This only goes green once the capture run above has published both assets and the manifest reflects `consumers: ['docs']` for both ids (already set in 3.1's registry entries). Acceptance: `apps/site/public/product/accounts-pending-light.png` and `apps/site/public/product/accounts-linked-light.png` exist; `apps/site/public/product/manifest.json` lists both shots; both `<ProductShot>` tags are embedded at the correct Steps in `dorkos-accounts.mdx`; the site docs-embed guard test passes. Verify: pnpm --filter @dorkos/e2e capture:record && pnpm --filter @dorkos/e2e capture:process && pnpm vitest run apps/site/src/layers/features/marketing/lib/__tests__/shots.test.ts && pnpm --filter @dorkos/site typecheck. Cites: spec Â§Detailed Design 6 (the two embed locations, exact `<ProductShot>` tags with alt text), Â§Detailed Design 5 ("Sequencing (load-bearing)" â€” the guard cannot pass until a real capture+process run has published the PNGs and manifest), Â§Implementation Phases ("Phase 4 â€” capture run + docs embeds"), Â§Technical Dependencies (`ProductShot` is global, no import line), Â§References (docs target + guard file:line citations). Depends on 3.1 (the drive and shard pin must exist before a capture run can produce these two assets).
tasks.activeForm: Running the capture record+process pipeline and embedding the two docs stills

tasks.phaseName: P5 â€” Close-out (draft ADR + follow-up pointer)
tasks.subject: [capture-cloud-link-stub] [P5] Extract the draft ADR via /adr:from-spec (accessor seam + fake honesty model + layered unreachability), record the flip-loop follow-up pointer for DONE â€” no changelog fragment (justified: zero product behavior change)
tasks.description: Close out the spec with its one durable design-decision artifact and a findable pointer for the follow-up the SPECIFY stage deliberately deferred â€” and explicitly do NOT add a changelog fragment. DRAFT ADR â€” run `/adr:from-spec specs/capture-cloud-link-stub/02-specification.md` (applies the significance rubric at extraction and writes `decisions/<id>-<slug>.md` + the `decisions/manifest.json` entry using a timestamp id from `.claude/scripts/id.ts`, `YYMMDD-HHMMSS`, e.g. matching the format of the most recent manifest entries); if the command is unavailable, write it by hand per the `writing-adrs` skill with the same content. Status: `proposed`. Title: capture the seam + the fake's honesty model â€” e.g. "Test-mode cloud transport: a DORKOS_TEST_RUNTIME-gated fake FetchLike behind a CloudLinkManager construction seam." Content, copied from spec Â§Related ADRs verbatim (all five items are load-bearing, not optional): (1) the honesty model â€” the fake fakes ONLY the `FetchLike` network dependency to `dorkos.ai`, exactly like `demo-scenarios.ts` fakes the agent backend; the real state machine, token persistence, all four `/api/cloud/*` routes, and the real client panel run unchanged; (2) the construction seam â€” reseat the eager `cloudLinkManager` singleton to an `init/getCloudLinkManager` accessor pair with a throwing guard, mirroring `get/setWorkspaceManager` (chosen over the `configManager` mutable-export pattern, which was considered and rejected as a foot-gun with no pre-init guard), so the composition root owns construction and production `cloud-link.ts` carries zero test branches; (3) the composition-root injection under the existing `DORKOS_TEST_RUNTIME` branch via dynamic `import()`, mirroring `TestModeRuntime`; (4) the layered unreachability proof â€” structural (dynamic import, absent from the prod module graph), runtime (the throwing guard on the validated `env.DORKOS_TEST_RUNTIME` boolean), and test (prod-default-construction + import-graph assertions); (5) the two-stills-for-MVP scope decision and the deferred flip-loop follow-up (see below). Cross-link `specs/accounts-and-auth`, `specs/cloud-account-management` (the shipped device-flow/persistence/heartbeat this spec photographs, unchanged), and the `TestModeRuntime`/`test-control.ts` precedent this seam mirrors. Add the `decisions/manifest.json` entry (status `proposed`, `extractedFrom`/`specSlug: capture-cloud-link-stub`, following the shape of the existing entries at the tail of that file). FOLLOW-UP POINTER (recorded here, NOT filed as a tracker issue â€” DECOMPOSE and EXECUTE do not write to the tracker; filing it is DONE stage's job per the `flow__closing-work` skill) â€” write a clearly labeled "Follow-up for DONE" note into this task's own completion summary (e.g. the PR description, or a short Consequences/Follow-ups section at the bottom of the draft ADR) naming the ONE deferred item so DONE has a concrete pointer instead of re-reading the full specification: **the flip loop.** Spec Â§Decisions resolved ("Open Q1 â€” two stills vs. a flip loop. RESOLVED: two docs stills for the MVP; the flip loop is a follow-up.") â€” a single `loop` of the in-place pendingâ†’linked flip is a more literal, more compelling reading than two stills, but it needs the fake to hold `pending` long enough to record the code and then flip inside the loop window, and it carries marketing weight plus a larger size budget than this spec's size-3 scope justified. The seam and fake built in 1.1/2.1 make the loop cheap once scoped: `PENDING_POLLS_BEFORE_APPROVAL` and the interval constants in `fake-cloud-link.ts` are exactly the knobs a loop drive would retune. NO CHANGELOG FRAGMENT (the honest call, per spec Â§Documentation) â€” this issue ships ZERO product behavior change: the pendingâ†’linked flip already worked and already shipped before this spec; the seam is a behavior-preserving refactor invisible to users; the fake is test-only; the only user-visible artifact is two screenshots added to an existing docs page. Do not create a `changelog/unreleased/*.md` fragment for this work â€” the repo's changelog documents what the product does for users, and the capturing-product-media pipeline routinely refreshes docs media without a fragment. (The steelman â€” "new docs images are user-visible, so a small Added entry" â€” is explicitly rejected in the spec: it would set a precedent that every screenshot refresh needs a fragment, and the user gains nothing new to do.) Acceptance: `decisions/<id>-<slug>.md` exists with status `proposed`, capturing all five content items above with the cross-links; `decisions/manifest.json` is valid JSON with the new entry; the flip-loop follow-up is recorded in a findable, explicitly labeled spot for DONE to pick up; NO changelog fragment was added for this spec. Verify: node -e "JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('decisions/manifest.json','utf8'))" (confirms the manifest still parses) && a manual read-through of the new ADR file against the five content items above. Cites: spec Â§Related ADRs (the full Proposed ADR content list, verbatim), Â§Decisions resolved ("Open Q1 â€” two stills vs. a flip loop. RESOLVED" â€” the follow-up's exact reasoning), Â§Documentation ("Changelog: NONE â€” the honest call", full rejection-of-the-steelman reasoning), Â§Implementation Phases ("Phase 5 â€” close-out"). Depends on 2.1 (the ADR documents the server seam + fake's exact honesty gate and construction pattern, which must be landed code â€” not just spec prose â€” to cite accurately) and 4.1 (the spec's own two-stills-vs-loop framing and the shipped docs surface are part of what the ADR and follow-up note describe; sequencing the close-out after the visible artifact exists matches the repo's own precedent of writing user-facing/decision prose after the surface it describes). No further parallelization â€” this is the closing task.
tasks.activeForm: Extracting the draft ADR and recording the flip-loop follow-up pointer
